subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Eris: The Troublemaker
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Eris: The Troublemaker.

hook.text: Eris is slightly smaller than Pluto but 27% more massive. Its discovery forced Pluto's demotion.

buildup.text: Mike Brown found Eris in 2005 orbiting far beyond Pluto. He announced it as the tenth planet.

discovery.text: If Eris was a planet, so were Ceres, Makemake, and more. The planet count would keep climbing.

twist.text: Eris was named after the Greek goddess of discord—fitting for the object that threw astronomy into chaos.

climax.text: Brown wrote a book called 'How I Killed Pluto.' The discovery reshaped our definition of a planet.

punchline.text: One icy world forced astronomy to rewrite its rules.

quiz.question: Why did the discovery of Eris lead to Pluto's reclassification?
options: Eris was similar in size, forcing astronomers to define what counts as a planet
Eris collided with Pluto and shattered it into pieces
Eris was found to be a lost moon of Neptune
